author: gurukamath
content:
Why can't we just charge the warm/cold gas for accessing the target upfront? Instead of splitting it into warm upfront + a cold surcharge later.
We don't need to access the state to determine if the target is cold

author: draganrakita
content:
> We don't need to access the state to determine if the target is cold 
This would be first time in evm this is done too, in revm is_warm is a flag inside accounts hashmap, it is more optimized if we can do it in same place (loading and warm check). We could move warm cost later when loading happens?

author: draganrakita
content: On one hand this is not a big deal, on other we are charging 100gas for tload/tstore that is basically a hashmap access. So i look at every hashmap access as 100gas.

author: gurukamath
content: Ah. Ok. The split access gas cost (warm upfront + cold surcharge later) makes sense in that case.

author: gurukamath
content: However, does this then mean, that all account accessing opcodes should follow this pattern? Also, would the delegation target access gas cost have to happen in two steps?
